en: In you, O LORD, do I take refuge; let me never be put to shame!
zh: 耶和華啊！我投靠你， 求你使我永不羞愧。

en: In your righteousness deliver me and rescue me; incline your ear to me, and save me!
zh: 求你按著你的公義搭救我，救贖我； 求你留心聽我，拯救我。

en: Be to me a rock of refuge, to which I may continually come; you have given the command to save me, for you are my rock and my fortress.
zh: 求你作我避難的磐石，使我可以常來投靠； 你已經下令救我， 因為你是我的巖石、我的堅壘。

en: Rescue me, O my God, from the hand of the wicked, from the grasp of the unjust and cruel man.
zh: 我的 神啊！求你救我脫離惡人的手， 脫離邪惡和殘暴的人的掌握，

en: For you, O Lord, are my hope, my trust, O LORD, from my youth.
zh: 因為你是我的盼望； 主耶和華啊！你是我自幼以來所倚靠的。

en: Upon you I have leaned from before my birth; you are he who took me from my mother's womb. My praise is continually of you.
zh: 我自出母胎就倚賴你， 把我從母腹中領出來的就是你， 我要常常讚美你。

en: I have been as a portent to many, but you are my strong refuge.
zh: 眾人都以我為怪， 但你是我堅固的避難所。

en: My mouth is filled with your praise, and with your glory all the day.
zh: 我要滿口讚美你， 我終日頌揚你的榮美。

en: Do not cast me off in the time of old age; forsake me not when my strength is spent.
zh: 我年老的時候，求你不要丟棄我； 我氣力衰弱的時候，求你不要離棄我。

en: For my enemies speak concerning me; those who watch for my life consult together
zh: 因為我的仇敵議論我； 那些窺探著要害我性命的，一同商議，

en: and say, "God has forsaken him; pursue and seize him, for there is none to deliver him."
zh: 說：“ 神已經離棄了他， 你們追趕他，拿住他吧！ 因為沒有人搭救他。”

en: O God, be not far from me; O my God, make haste to help me!
zh: 神啊！求你不要遠離我； 我的 神啊！求你快來幫助我。

en: May my accusers be put to shame and consumed; with scorn and disgrace may they be covered who seek my hurt.
zh: 願那些控告我的， 都羞愧滅亡； 願那些謀求害我的， 都蒙羞受辱。

en: But I will hope continually and will praise you yet more and more.
zh: 至於我，我要常常仰望你， 要多多讚美你。

en: My mouth will tell of your righteous acts, of your deeds of salvation all the day, for their number is past my knowledge.
zh: 我的口要述說你的公義， 終日講述你的救恩， 儘管我不知道它們的數量。

en: With the mighty deeds of the Lord GOD I will come; I will remind them of your righteousness, yours alone.
zh: 我要來述說主耶和華大能的事； 我要提說你獨有的公義。

en: O God, from my youth you have taught me, and I still proclaim your wondrous deeds.
zh: 神啊！我自幼以來，你就教導我； 直到現在，我還是宣揚你奇妙的作為。

en: So even to old age and gray hairs, O God, do not forsake me, until I proclaim your might to another generation, your power to all those to come.
zh: 神啊！到我年老髮白的時候， 求你仍不要離棄我， 等我把你的能力向下一代傳揚， 把你的大能向後世所有的人傳揚。

en: Your righteousness, O God, reaches the high heavens. You who have done great things, O God, who is like you?
zh: 神啊！你的公義達到高天， 你曾經行過大事， 神啊！有誰像你呢？

en: You who have made me see many troubles and calamities will revive me again; from the depths of the earth you will bring me up again.
zh: 你使我們經歷了很多苦難， 你必使我們再活過來， 你必把我們從地的深處救上來。

en: You will increase my greatness and comfort me again.
zh: 你必增加我的尊榮， 並轉過來安慰我。

en: I will also praise you with the harp for your faithfulness, O my God; I will sing praises to you with the lyre, O Holy One of Israel.
zh: 我的 神啊！ 我要彈琴稱讚你，稱讚你的信實； 以色列的聖者啊！ 我要鼓瑟歌頌你。

en: My lips will shout for joy, when I sing praises to you; my soul also, which you have redeemed.
zh: 我歌頌你的時候，我的嘴唇要歡呼； 我的靈魂，就是你所救贖的，也要歡呼。

en: And my tongue will talk of your righteous help all the day long, for they have been put to shame and disappointed who sought to do me hurt.
zh: 我的舌頭也要終日講述你的公義， 因為那些謀求害我的人已經蒙羞受辱了。
